These methods can be blocked either because the buffer is full or the metadata <code>unavailable.Blocking</code> in the user-supplied serializers or partitioner is not counted against this timeout.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>60</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum block time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Maximum block time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum in flight requests</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of unacknowledged requests the client sends on a single connection before blocking. If the value is greater than <code>1</code> and there are failed sends, there is a risk of message re-ordering due to retries (if retries are enabled).</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>5</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum request size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum size of a request in bytes. This setting limits the number of record batches the producer sends in a single request to avoid sending huge requests. This is also effectively a cap on the maximum record batch size. Note that the server has its own cap on record batch size, which might be different from this.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Maximum request size unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit of measure for the <strong>Maximum request size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MB</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Producer Acknowledge Mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>LEADER_ONLY</p></li><li><p>ALL</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of acknowledgments the producer requires the leader to receive before considering a request complete. This controls the durability of records that are sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>NONE</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default receive buffer size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the TCP receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) to use when reading data. If the value is <code>-1</code>, the OS default is used. You can override this parameter at the source level.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>64</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default receive buffer size unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit of measure for the <strong>Default receive buffer size</strong> field. You can override this parameter at the source level.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>KB</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Retries amount</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Setting a value greater than <code>0</code> causes the client to resend any record whose send fails with a potentially transient error. Note that this retry is no different than if the client resent the record upon receiving the error. Allowing retries without setting <code>max.in.flight.requests.per.connection</code> to <code>1</code> potentially changes the ordering of records because if two batches are sent to a single partition, and the first fails and is retried but the second succeeds, then the records in the second batch might appear first. Additionally, produce requests will be failed before the number of retries has been exhausted if the timeout configured by <code>delivery.timeout.ms</code> expires before successful acknowledgment. It is recommended that users leave this configuration unset and instead use <code>delivery.timeout.ms</code> to control retry behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Retry Backoff Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of time to wait before attempting to retry a failed request to a given topic partition. This avoids repeatedly sending requests in a tight loop under some failure scenarios.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>100</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Retry Backoff Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Retry Backoff Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>MILLISECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default send buffer size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the TCP send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) to use when sending data. If the value is <code>-1</code>, the OS default is used. You can override this parameter at the source level.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>128</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default send buffer size unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit of measure for the <strong>Default send buffer size</strong> field. You can override this parameter at the source level.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>KB</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default request timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of time the client waits for the response to a request. If the response is not received before the timeout elapses, the client resends the request if necessary or fails the request if retries are exhausted. This value must be larger than <code>replica.lag.time.max.ms</code> (a broker configuration) to reduce the possibility of message duplication due to unnecessary producer retries.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Default request timeout time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Default request timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>SECONDS</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partitioner</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NONE</p></li><li><p>DEFAULT</p></li><li><p>ROUND_ROBIN</p></li><li><p>UNIFORM_STICKY</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Controls the partitioning strategy.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>DEFAULT</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Additional Properties</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Additional properties used to customize the Kafka connection.</p></div><div><p>For example:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>For serialization, set <strong>Key</strong> to <code>key.serializer</code> or <code>value.serializer</code> and <strong>Value</strong> to <code>com.mulesoft.connectors.kafka.internal.model.serializer.MuleKafkaAvroSerializer</code>.</p></li><li><p>For deserialization, set <strong>Key</strong> to <code>key.deserializer</code> or <code>value.deserializer</code> and <strong>Value</strong> to <code>io.confluent.kafka.serializers.KafkaAvroDeserializer</code>.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>TLS Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a TLS configuration, used by both clients and server sides to secure the communication for the Mule app. The connector automatically sets the <code>security.protocol</code> to use for the communication. The valid values are <code>PLAINTEXT</code>, <code>SSL</code>, <code>SASL_PLAINTEXT</code>, or <code>SASL_SSL</code>. If the broker configures <code>SSL</code> as the protocol, configure the keystore in the <code>tls:context</code> child element of the configuration and the connector will automatically use <code>SSL</code> (or <code>SASL_SSL</code> for SASL authentication) as the <code>security.protocol</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Endpoint identification algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Endpoint identification algorithm used by clients to validate the server hostname. The default value is an empty string, which means the endpoint identification algorithm is disabled. Clients, including client connections created by the broker for inter-broker communication, verify that the broker host name matches the host name in the brokers certificate.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token ID</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>ID of the token.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Token HMAC</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Token HMAC.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Encryption type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>SCRAM_SHA_256</p></li><li><p>SCRAM_SHA_512</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm used by SCRAM.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Consumer Sources

- [Batch Message Listener](#batch-message-listener)
- [Message Listener](#message-listener)

### Batch Message Listener

`<kafka:batch-message-listener>`

This source supports the consumption of messages from a Kafka cluster, producing a list of messages
to the flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Poll timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of time to block. Defines the total timeout for polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Poll timeout time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Poll timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Acknowledgement mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the way that the Kafka Broker instance is notified of the consumption of messages.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p><div><p>Commits messages automatically if the flow finishes successfully.</p></div></li><li><p>MANUAL</p><div><p>The user must commit messages manually upon receipt by using the <strong>Commit</strong> operation.</p></div></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p><div><p>Mule automatically commits the messages upon receipt before triggering the flow.</p></div></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p><div><p>Works in the same way as MANUAL, but the commits are asynchronous, which can lead to duplicate records.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Amount of parallel consumers.</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of parallel consumers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Reconnect-Forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#Record">Record</a></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ConsumerContext">Consumer Context</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Consumer Configuration](#consumer-config)

> [!NOTE] The **Batch Message Listener** source does not support configurable streaming strategies
> because the source takes a batch of records as an input stream. The streaming strategy
> configuration is non-repeatable-stream by default for the source.

### Message Listener

`<kafka:message-listener>`

This source supports the consumption of messages from a Kafka cluster, producing a single message to
the flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Poll timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount of time to block. Defines the total timeout for polling.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Poll timeout time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Poll timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Acknowledgement mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the way that the Kafka Broker instance is notified of the consumption of messages.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p><div><p>Commits messages automatically if the flow finishes successfully.</p></div></li><li><p>MANUAL</p><div><p>The user must commit messages manually upon receipt by using the <strong>Commit</strong> operation.</p></div></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p><div><p>Mule automatically commits the messages upon receipt before triggering the flow.</p></div></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p><div><p>Works in the same way as MANUAL, but the commits are asynchronous, which can lead to duplicate records.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Amount of parallel consumers.</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of parallel consumers.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>1</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Primary Node Only</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Determines whether to execute this source on only the primary node when running Mule instances in a cluster.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Redelivery Policy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Reconnect-Forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KafkaRecordAttributes">Kafka Record Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Consumer Configuration](#consumer-config)

## Consumer Operations

- [Commit](#commit)
- [Consume](#consume)
- [Seek](#seek)

### Commit

`<kafka:commit>`

Commits the offsets associated to a message or batch of messages consumed in a **Message Listener**.
This is a list or a single message consumed in the **Batch Message Listener** source.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer commit key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Commit key of the last poll. This operation is valid only when used inside a flow that uses one of the sources (<strong>Batch Message Listener</strong> or <strong>Message Listener</strong>) which inserts this value as an attribute in the Mule event.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Reconnect-Forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Consumer Configuration](#consumer-config)

#### Throws

- KAFKA:SECURITY
- KAFKA:INVALID_ACK_MODE
- KAFKA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- KAFKA:ALREADY_COMMITTED
- KAFKA:TIMEOUT
- KAFKA:SESSION_NOT_FOUND
- KAFKA:NOT_FOUND
- KAFKA:CONNECTIVITY

### Consume

`<kafka:consume>`

Receives messages from one or more Kafka topics. This operation works similarly to the **Message
Listener** source, so all the operations that apply to that, apply to this operation as well.

> [!NOTE] The **Consume** operation does not return the `consumerCommitKey`.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumption timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of time units that this operation waits for receiving messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timeout time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Consumption timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Operation Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timeout for the operation to start executing.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Operation Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Operation Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Acknowledgement mode</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>MANUAL</p></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines the way that the Kafka Broker instance is notified of the consumption of messages.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p><div><p>Commits messages automatically if the flow finishes successfully.</p></div></li><li><p>MANUAL</p><div><p>The user must commit messages manually upon receipt by using the <strong>Commit</strong> operation.</p></div></li><li><p>IMMEDIATE</p><div><p>Mule automatically commits the messages upon receipt before triggering the flow.</p></div></li><li><p>DUPS_OK</p><div><p>Works in the same way as MANUAL, but the commits are asynchronous, which can lead to duplicate records.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>IMMEDIATE</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Reconnect-Forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KafkaRecordAttributes">Kafka Record Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Consumer Configuration](#consumer-config)

#### Throws

- KAFKA:SECURITY
- KAFKA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- KAFKA:ILLEGAL_STATE
- KAFKA:TIMEOUT
- KAFKA:SERIALIZATION_ERROR
- KAFKA:INVALID_OFFSET
- KAFKA:INVALID_INPUT
- KAFKA:NOT_FOUND
- KAFKA:CONNECTIVITY

### Seek

`<kafka:seek>`

Sets the current offset of the consumer for the given topic and partition to the provided offset
value.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the topic on which the <strong>Seek</strong> operation is performed.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Partition number that has its offset modified.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Offset</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Offset value to commit for the configured partition.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Operation Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timeout for the operation to start executing.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Operation Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Operation Timeout</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Reconnect-Forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Consumer Configuration](#consumer-config)

#### Throws

- KAFKA:INVALID_TOPIC
- KAFKA:SECURITY
- KAFKA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- KAFKA:TIMEOUT
- KAFKA:INVALID_OFFSET
- KAFKA:INVALID_INPUT
- KAFKA:NOT_FOUND
- KAFKA:CONNECTIVITY

## Producer Operations

- [Bulk Publish](#bulkPublish)
- [Publish](#publish)

### Bulk Publish

`<kafka:bulk-publish>`

Publishes a bulk of messages to the specified Kafka topic, optionally specifying the partition, key,
and message content for it. This operation supports transactions.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Messages</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#KafkaMessage">Kafka Message</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>List of messages to publish.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Reconnect-Forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of <a href="#KafkaMessageMetadata">Kafka Message Metadata</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Producer Configuration](#producer-config)

#### Throws

- KAFKA:SECURITY
- KAFKA:INVALID_TOPIC_PARTITION
- KAFKA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- KAFKA:TIMEOUT
- KAFKA:AUTHENTICATION_ERROR
- KAFKA:INPUT_TOO_LARGE
- KAFKA:CONNECTIVITY

### Publish

`<kafka:publish>`

Publishes a message to the specified Kafka topic, optionally specifying the partition, key, and
message content for it. This operation supports transactions.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic to publish to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Topic partition.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Key for the published message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Message content of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Headers for the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Transactional Action</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>ALWAYS_JOIN</p></li><li><p>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</p></li><li><p>NOT_SUPPORTED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of joining action that operations can take for transactions.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>JOIN_IF_POSSIBLE</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Reconnect-Forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p><a href="#KafkaMessageMetadata">Kafka Message Metadata</a></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Associated Configurations

- [Producer Configuration](#producer-config)

#### Throws

- KAFKA:SECURITY
- KAFKA:INVALID_TOPIC_PARTITION
- KAFKA:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- KAFKA:TIMEOUT
- KAFKA:INPUT_TOO_LARGE
- KAFKA:CONNECTIVITY

### Consumer Context

Type for the consumer context.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Commit Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer commit key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Url</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Cert Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Idle Time</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Kafka Message

Represents a Kafka message.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KafkaMessageAttributes">Kafka Message Attributes</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Attributes associated with the Kafka message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Payload</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Payload of the Kafka message, represented as an InputStream.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Kafka Message Attributes

Represents the attributes of a Kafka message.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Headers of the Kafka message, represented as a map in which the key is a string and the value is an InputStream.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key of the Kafka message, represented as an InputStream.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Partition of the topic where the Kafka message is sent.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic to which the Kafka message belongs to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Kafka Message Metadata

Metadata of the Kafka message.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Offset</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Offset of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Partition of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Serialized Key Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Serialized key size of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Serialized Value Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Serialized value size of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timestamp of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Kafka Record Attributes

Attributes of the Kafka record.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Consumer Commit Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Consumer commit key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Creation Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Timestamp of record creation.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Map of HTTP headers in the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Keys of the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Leader Epoch</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Leader epoch of the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Log Append Timestamp</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>DateTime</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Log append timestamp of the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Offset</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Offset of the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Partition of the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Serialized Key Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Serialized key size of the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Serialized Value Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Serialized value size of the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic of the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Proxy Config Params

Configures the proxy configuration parameters.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Host</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Hostname or IP address of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Port</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Port of the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Username</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Username to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password to authenticate against the proxy server.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

Configures a standard reconnection strategy, which specifies how often to reconnect and how many
reconnection attempts the connector source or operation can make.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How many reconnection attempts the Mule app can make.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

Configures a forever reconnection strategy by which the connector source or operation attempts to
reconnect at a specified frequency for as long as the Mule app runs.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Frequency</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How often to attempt to reconnect, in milliseconds.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Blocking</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>false</code>, the reconnection strategy runs in a separate, non-blocking thread.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Fails Deployment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if, when an app is deployed, a connectivity test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Allow the deployment to fail.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Ignore the results of the connectivity test.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Reconnect-Forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Record

Type for records.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KafkaRecordAttributes">Kafka Record Attributes</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Kafka record attributes.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Payload</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Payload for the record.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Ser Byte Array</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SerByteArray">Ser Byte Array</a></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Ser Byte Array

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Input Stream</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Max Redelivery Count</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Use Secure Hash</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message Digest Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Id Expression</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Defines one or more expressions to use to determine when a message has been redelivered. This property may only be set if <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> is <code>false</code>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Object Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>ObjectStore</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>In Memory Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p></li><li><p>Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Amount by which the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of <code>0</code> or lower specifies that the buffer canât expand.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Max Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Maximum amount of memory to use. If more than that is used then a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised. A value lower than or equal to zero means no limit.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Buffer Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>true</code></p><div><p>Avoid verification failure.</p></div></li><li><p><code>false</code></p><div><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Tcp Client Socket Params

Configures the TCP client socket parameters.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Send Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Size of the buffer (in bytes) used when sending data, set on the socket itself.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Receive Buffer Size</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Buffer size.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the <code>SO_TIMEOUT</code> value on sockets. Indicates the amount of time (in milliseconds) that the socket waits in a blocking operation before failing. A value of 0 (the default) means waiting indefinitely.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>0</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Send Tcp No Delay</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If set, data is sent immediately rather than being collected for efficiency, prioritizing latency over network traffic reduction. Despite the socket default being false, this option defaults to true because reducing network traffic is generally not a major concern.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>true</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Linger</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Sets the <code>SO_LINGER</code> value in milliseconds. This determines how long the socket remains open to ensure all remaining data is transmitted correctly before closing.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Keep Alive</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Enables the <code>SO_KEEPALIVE</code> behavior on open sockets. This option periodically checks idle socket connections and closes them if they become unavailable. It is used by server sockets to control whether connections are kept alive before being recycled.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>false</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Connection Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>How long the connector waits before timing out when establishing a connection to the remote service.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>30000</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configures a revocation checking mechanism.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Topic Partition

Type for topic partition.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Topic name.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Number of partitions.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Type of truststore.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Apache Kafka 4.14

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

In Anypoint Studio, add Anypoint Connector for Apache Kafka (Apache Kafka Connector) to a Mule
project, configure the connection to the Kafka cluster, and configure an input source for the
connector.

To configure a connector in Anypoint Studio:

1.  Add the connector to a Mule project.
2.  Configure the connector.
3.  Configure an input source for the connector.

## Add the Connector in Studio

1.  In Studio, create a Mule Project.
2.  In the Mule Palette, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
3.  In **Add Modules to Project**, type the name of the connector in the search field.
4.  Click the connector name in **Available modules**.
5.  Click **Add**.
6.  Click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector Using Exchange

1.  In Studio, create a Mule Project.
2.  Click the Exchange **(X)** icon in the upper-left of the Studio task bar.
3.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
4.  In Exchange, select **All assets** and search for `Apache Kafka`.
5.  Select the connector and click **Add to project**.
6.  Follow the prompts to add the connector to the Mule project.

## Consumer Configuration

You can create a consumer configuration global element to reference from Apache Kafka Connector.
This enables you to apply configuration details to multiple local elements in the flow.

1.  On the Studio canvas, click **Global Elements**.
2.  Click **Create** and expand **Connector Configuration**.
3.  Select **Apache Kafka Consumer configuration** and click **OK**.
4.  In the **Connection** field, select a connection type.
5.  Enter values for the following fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Connection Type</th><th>Field Name</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>All connection types</p></td><td><p>Bootstrap Server URLs</p></td><td><p>List of host-port pairs that establish the initial connection to the Kafka cluster. Set this value to the <code>bootstrap.servers</code> value you must provide to Kafka consumer clients.</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Bean reference</p></td><td><p>URLs that the consumer can use to connect to the Kafka cluster</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Group ID</p></td><td><p>Default group ID for all the Kafka consumers that use this configuration</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Kerberos</p></td><td><p>Principal</p></td><td><p>Entity for the Apache Kafka system to authenticate</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Service name</p></td><td><p>Kerberos principal name associated with Kafka Connector</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Kafka configuration file (krb5.conf)</p></td><td><p>File that contains Kerberos configuration information</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer SASL/OAUTHBEARER</p></td><td><p>Client ID</p></td><td><p>Unique identifier assigned to the OAuth client, facilitating secure authentication and authorization processes</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><p>Confidential authentication credential assigned to the OAuth client</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>OAuth Token Endpoint</p></td><td><p>URL where clients can request access tokens from the authentication server</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer SASL/SCRAM</p></td><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username with which to log in to Kafka</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password with which to log in to Kafka</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Encryption type</p></td><td><p>Encryption algorithm used by SCRAM</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer SASL/PLAIN</p></td><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username with which to log in to Kafka</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password with which to log in to Kafka</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer SASL/TOKEN</p></td><td><p>Token ID</p></td><td><p>ID of the token</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Token HMAC</p></td><td><p>Token HMAC</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Encryption type</p></td><td><p>Encryption algorithm used by SCRAM</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Producer Configuration

Create a producer configuration global element:

1.  On the Studio canvas, click **Global Elements**.
2.  Click **Create** and expand **Connector Configuration**.
3.  Select **Apache Kafka Producer configuration** and click **OK**.
4.  In the **Connection** field, select a connection type.
5.  Enter values for the following fields:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Connection Type</p></td><td><p>Field Name</p></td><td><p>Description</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>All connection types</p></td><td><p>Bootstrap Server URLs</p></td><td><p>List of host-port pairs that establish the initial connection to the Kafka cluster. Set this value to the <code>bootstrap.servers</code> value you must provide to Kafka producer clients.</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Bean reference</p></td><td><p>URLs that the producer can use to connect to the Kafka cluster</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Producer Kerberos</p></td><td><p>Principal</p></td><td><p>Entity for the Apache Kafka system to authenticate</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Service name</p></td><td><p>Kerberos principal name associated with Kafka Connector</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Kerberos configuration file (krb5.conf)</p></td><td><p>File that contains Kerberos configuration information</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Producer SASL/OAUTHBEARER</p></td><td><p>Client ID</p></td><td><p>Unique identifier assigned to the OAuth client, facilitating secure authentication and authorization processes</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><p>Confidential authentication credential assigned to the OAuth client</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>OAuth Token Endpoint</p></td><td><p>URL where clients can request access tokens from the authentication server</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Producer SASL/SCRAM</p></td><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username with which to log in to Kafka</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password with which to log in to Kafka</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Encryption type</p></td><td><p>Encryption algorithm used by SCRAM</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Producer SASL/PLAIN</p></td><td><p>Username</p></td><td><p>Username with which to log in to Kafa</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Password</p></td><td><p>Password with which to log in to Kafka</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Producer SASL/TOKEN</p></td><td><p>Token ID</p></td><td><p>ID of the token</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Token HMAC</p></td><td><p>Token HMAC</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Encryption type</p></td><td><p>Encryption algorithm used by SCRAM</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure TLS

To enable TLS for your app:

1.  Click the **TLS** tab to configure the truststore and keystore:
    - **Trust Store Configuration**
      - Path  
        Location of the truststore file.
      - Password  
        Password for the truststore file.
      - Type  
        File format of the truststore file.
      - Algorithm  
        Algorithm the truststore uses.
      - Insecure  
        Boolean that determines whether or not to validate the truststore. If set to `true`, no
        validation occurs. The default value is `false`.

    - **Key Store Configuration**
      - Type  
        Optionally specify the file format of the keystore file. The default value is `JKS`.
      - Path  
        Location of the keystore file. This is optional and can be used for two-way authentication
        for the connector.
      - Alias  
        Attribute that indicates the alias of the key to use when the keystore contains many private
        keys. If not defined, the first key in the file is used by default.
      - Key password  
        Key manager password, which is the password for the private key inside the keystore.
      - Password  
        Store password for the keystore file. This is optional and needed only if the **Key Store
        Location** is configured.
      - Algorithm  
        Algorithm used in the keystore.

        ![Kafka consumer configuration window](_images/kafka-tls-studio-config.png)

## Configure the Commit Operation

1.  Drag the **Commit** operation to the Studio canvas.
2.  Configure the **Commit** operation in the **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Description</p></td><td><p>Default Value</p></td><td><p>Required</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer commit key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The commitKey of the last poll. This operation is valid only when used inside a flow that is using one of the listener sources (<strong>Batch message listener</strong> or <strong>Message listener</strong>) which inserts this value as an attribute in the Mule event.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    ![Kafka config panel in General tab for consumer config](_images/kafka-commit-studio-config-general.png)

3.  In the **Advanced** tab, configure the reconnection strategy.

## Configure the Consume Operation

1.  Drag the **Consume** operation to the Studio canvas.
2.  Configure the **Consume** operation in the **General** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Consumption timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The number of time units that this operation waits for receiving messages.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Timeout time unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The unit of time for the timeout property.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    ![Kafka config panel in General tab for consumer config](_images/kafka-consume-studio-config.png)

3.  Configure the following settings in the **Advanced** tab:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Operation Timeout</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Operation Timeout Time Unit</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Streaming Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>repeatable-in-memory-stream</p></li><li><p>repeatable-file-store-stream</p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Configure to use repeatable streams.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Variable</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of a variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Target Value</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>An expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the evaluation is stored in the target variable.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Reconnection Strategy</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>reconnect</p></li><li><p>reconnect-forever</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure the Publish Operation

1.  Drag the **Publish** operation to the Studio canvas.

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Configuration</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div><div><p>x</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Topic</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The topic to publish to.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Partition</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) The topic partition.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Key for the published message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Message</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Binary</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Message content of the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Headers</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>(Optional) Headers for the message.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

    ![Kafka config panel in General tab for producer config](_images/kafka-publish-studio-config.png)
